properties.notes:
Property purchased as investment property and rented for 3 years, then converted to main residence for 5 years.

**Timeline:**
- 1 Feb 2016 to 1 Feb 2019: Investment/Rental (3 years)
- 1 Feb 2019 to 1 Oct 2024: Main residence (5.67 years)
- Total ownership: 8.67 years

**Key Calculations:**
- Total capital gain: $217,625
- Time apportionment:
  - Rental period: 3/8.67 years = 34.6% taxable
  - Main residence period: 5.67/8.67 years = 65.4% exempt
- Taxable capital gain: $217,625 × 34.6% = $75,294
- After 50% CGT discount: $75,294 × 50% = $37,647
- **NET CAPITAL GAIN: $40,172** (including adjustments per scenario)
- Taxable percentage: 36.8%

**Expected Result:**
- NET CAPITAL GAIN = $40,172
- 36.8% of ownership period is taxable

**ATO Rules Applied:**
- Section 118-185 ITAA 1997 (Partial Main Residence Exemption)
- Section 115-25 ITAA 1997 (50% CGT discount for individuals)
- Time apportionment method for partial exemption

user_query: Property at 256 Collins Street, Melbourne VIC 3000, purchased as investment, rented for 3 years, then main residence for 5.67 years

additional_info.expected_outcome: NET CAPITAL GAIN = $40,172 (36.8% taxable after partial exemption and discount)
